source: Zero-length HKDF salt (RFC 5869 section 2.2 absent salt). When salt is a zero-length octet string, HKDF-Extract substitutes a string of HashLen zero bytes. This case isolates that semantic with a non-empty info string, mirroring the slots_mac HKDF call in the sealed-PoE construction (salt is the empty octet string, info is the domain-separation label), so the absent-salt behaviour is pinned by an exact byte vector rather than left to the library. The RFC 5869 A.3 KAT in hkdf-sha256-kat.json also uses salt='' but pairs it with an empty info; this file pins salt='' against a non-empty info so the two inputs cannot mask each other.
